commit.message:
fix(audit): redact sensitive audit payloads

Summary:
- redact sensitive tool-call arguments before writing audit summaries or details
- stop persisting raw tool outputs in audit events and replace them with metadata-only result summaries
- add audit formatting tests covering argument redaction and output-safe summaries

Rationale:
- address PR review findings that audit logging could expose secrets from tool inputs and outputs
- keep the trust trail useful without turning the audit API or settings panel into a secret leak path

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/audit/formatting.py backend/src/api/ws.py backend/tests/test_audit_formatting.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_audit_formatting.py tests/test_audit_api.py tests/test_e2e_conversation.py
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: seraph
repository.description: AI agent with a retro 16-bit RPG village UI — Phaser 3 canvas, persistent identity, long-term memory, hierarchical goals, and plug-and-play MCP server integration

commit.message:
feat(audit): log tool activity

Summary:
- add a structured audit event store and API for recent tool activity
- write tool call and tool result events from the WebSocket agent flow with session, policy mode, and risk metadata
- surface the latest audit trail in the settings panel so trust-boundary events are visible in the app

Rationale:
- continue Season 1 trust boundaries work by making meaningful agent actions queryable and reviewable
- build on the new tool policy modes with an audit trail users can inspect without digging through chat step text

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/audit/repository.py backend/src/api/audit.py backend/src/api/ws.py backend/src/tools/policy.py backend/tests/test_audit_api.py backend/tests/test_e2e_conversation.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_audit_api.py tests/test_e2e_conversation.py tests/test_settings_api.py tests/test_settings_capture_mode.py tests/test_settings_tool_policy_mode.py tests/test_tool_registry.py tests/test_tools_api.py
- cd frontend && npm test
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: シフト調整チャットのコンテキスト日付検証を強化

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(settings): add tool policy mode

Summary:
- add persisted tool policy modes with backend filtering for native and delegated tools
- expose tool policy mode in settings and tool metadata APIs with SQLite compatibility for existing profiles
- add a settings panel toggle that refreshes the visible tool registry after changes

Rationale:
- start the Season 1 trust and capability work with a concrete control over agent execution breadth
- give users a safer default path to restrict shell, vault, and MCP access without removing the current full-power mode

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_settings_api.py tests/test_settings_capture_mode.py tests/test_settings_tool_policy_mode.py tests/test_tool_registry.py tests/test_tools_api.py
- cd frontend && npm test
- git diff --cached --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: シフト調整チャットのコンテキスト検証を強化

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(workflow): tighten Linear comment guidance

Summary:
- clarify when the workflow prompt should use linear_graphql versus the dedicated comment tool
- require falling back to linear_graphql for completion comments only when the dedicated tool is unavailable
- extend the repository workflow test to cover the new prompt rule

Rationale:
- the workflow prompt needs a clear preference order so agents do not overuse the generic GraphQL tool
- the requested tracker credential and project slug edits were intentionally left out of this commit

Tests:
- not run separately in this step

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: Web起動シーケンスとSentryビルド設定を調整

Sentry初期化後にAppを遅延読み込みするbootstrap処理へ変更し、型安全に起動できるよう修正。
Sentry Viteプラグインを本番ビルド時のみ有効化し、アップロード後のsourcemap削除設定を追加。
READMEのSentry説明を実装挙動に合わせて更新。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore: 生成物ディレクトリを静的解析と整形対象から除外

oxfmt と oxlint の ignorePatterns に coverage/dist/node_modules/storybook-static を追加し、生成物で web:check が失敗しないように調整。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(test): avoid excluded phase banners

Summary:
- move progress phase reporting to real test-start events instead of
  module-start events
- ignore excluded and skipped tests when deciding whether to print a
  phase banner

Rationale:
- keep the custom test progress output aligned with what ExUnit
  actually runs
- avoid implying that hardware tests executed when they were only
  loaded and excluded

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/hardware/ring_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs:124

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(output): quiet mix test logging

Summary:
- capture logger output during ExUnit runs so passing tests stay quiet
- add a formatter that prints one-line unit and integration phase
  markers
- capture the expected simulator crash log in the crash-path test

Rationale:
- keep mix test output focused on progress and failures instead of
  runtime log noise
- preserve detailed logs for failed tests without hiding real failure
  context

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs:124
- mix test test/ethercat test/telemetry_test.exs
  test/integration/support/scenario_test.exs
- mix test (4 raw transport timeout failures remain in
  test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(runtime): remove duplicated helper logic

Summary:
- extract shared simulator slave list and memory helpers to remove
  repeated update, fetch, bit, and binary replacement code
- consolidate common WKC/result checks in EtherCAT.Utils and simplify
  master, slave, and mailbox activation and recovery flows
- keep workflow-specific refactors local for activation, mailbox writes,
  and step runners while preserving existing behavior

Rationale:
- the repo had a cluster of exact clones across simulator, slave, and
  master runtime modules that added maintenance overhead
- narrow shared helpers keep the deduplication cohesive without adding
  another generic API utils layer
- local extractions remove repetition where reuse is intra-module only,
  which keeps boundaries explicit

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test --warnings-as-errors
- mix ex_dna

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(changelog): fill unreleased hash references

Summary:
- replace pending unreleased hash markers with the short hash for
  the release-surface hardening commit

Rationale:
- AGENTS.md requires release-note-worthy changelog entries to carry
  short hashes before handoff
- keep the unreleased section ready for release note extraction

Validation:
- not run (changelog-only update)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(repo): harden release surface and capture safety

Summary:
- move simulator reference notes out of the tracked lib tree and
  point simulator docs at local helper material
- make capture artifacts data-only .capture files and reject
  malformed payloads without evaluating code
- normalize public API and simulator call exits to return
  {:error, {:server_exit, reason}} and align release workflow and
  release metadata with raw-capable CI

Rationale:
- keep packaged artifacts reproducible and avoid shipping local
  helper material or executable capture files
- preserve caller safety when local servers die mid-call and ensure
  tag releases exercise the same transport surface as CI
- record the post-v0.3.1 work in the changelog before release prep

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test --warnings-as-errors
- mix format --check-formatted
- mix docs.fresh
- mix hex.build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

ci: merge security checks into defense for #132

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex (GPT-5) <codex@openai.com>

Libdl: In `Libdl.dlpath`, throw an ArgumentError if the handle is `C_NULL` (#61307)

🤖 Discovered by Codex during an audit.

Other functions in Libdl (such as `Libdl.dlsym`) already include a check
for `C_NULL`. For example:


https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/bc4bb7ae9a49808a929917d921e597906a9f30cb/base/libdl.jl#L59-L60

So this PR adds a `C_NULL` check to `Libdl.dlpath`, and (similar to
`Libdl.dlsym`), throws an `ArgumentError`.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix invalid date when saving today's weight

Wire the Today card weight buttons through a real function call so the click event is no longer passed into openWeightModal as the date value. Also add a defensive date-format guard inside openWeightModal so invalid callers fall back to today instead of saving a bad date to Supabase.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

rename frame partial-state predicate

Use has_partial? instead of finished_cleanly? so the reader exposes its concrete state and the worker-pool error check reads directly as a transport-policy decision.

commit.message:
move frame EOF policy to worker pool

Replace ParallelFrameReader#finish! with a simple finished_cleanly? predicate so the reader reports state and the worker-pool caller decides that trailing bytes at EOF are a transport error.

unify runner input ownership

Make the CLI runner own file inputs in both serial and parallel paths instead of threading file_paths separately through parallel-only APIs.

This removes a misleading seam from the PR and keeps the runner interface aligned with its internal state.
